title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

This choropleth world map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for 2019, based on a statistical model that combines measures from several other sources. The legend ranges from -4 to 4, where large positive scores indicate that abuses are rare and large negative scores indicate that abuses are relatively widespread. The color scale transitions from dark red at -4, through orange and beige near zero, to light and dark blue at 4. Countries in the darkest blue band, representing scores near 4, include Canada, Norway, Sweden, Finland, New Zealand, and Uruguay. A broader group of nations, including the United States, Australia, Japan, and most of Western Europe, sits in the lighter blue bands between 1 and 3. Conversely, the darkest red shades, corresponding to scores near -4, appear in Yemen, Sudan, and Myanmar. Surrounding regions in North Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Asia display various shades of orange, indicating negative scores between -1 and -3. Large portions of central Africa and Greenland are marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data. The visual distribution shows the highest scores concentrated in North America, Western Europe, and Oceania, while the lowest scores are clustered in specific areas of the Middle East, Africa, and Southeast Asia.
